documents.title: RAFFLE LICENSES 12.20.22
EMS FIRE APPARATUS MAINT AND REPAIR RESOLUTION 2022

pages.text: 320-22  December 20, 2022  RESOLUTION TO AWARD CONTRACT  FOR   FIRE   EMS   APPARATUS MAINTENANCE AND REPAIR  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange   Fire Department   has requested the services of a  company to   perform routine maintenance and repair on   a   various number of equipment   to keep it  operating effic iently, see Exhibit A; and  WHEREAS ,   in accordance with the   Local Public Contract Law N.J.S.A. 40A:11- 1 et seq., the  Purchasing Agent advertised for   bids and did receive   one   (1)   submission   on the   date   December 6,   2022,  and time   11:00 am   posted;   and  WHEREAS   one bid was   received,   and that bid was reviewed by   both   the West Orange Fire  department   and   the Purchasing Agent   to be   found   responsible and respon sive   to the specifications   and  cost   restraints:   and  WHEREAS   the following   proposal has been submitted by:  VCI Emergency Vehicle Specialists, LLC  43 Jefferson Ave  Berlin, NJ 08009  Annual   preventive   maintenance costs:  Chassis   $ 450.00   per   5k mile   interval $900.00 per 30k interval , 5 vehicles 5k $2250.00, 5 vehicles 30k  $4500.00  Patient Compartment/Module As needed per wear & tear that is not included in the warranty  General Repairs Rates:  Hourly Repair Rate $ 150.00  Hourly OT $ 150.00  Travel Cost $ 0  OEM Parts Mark Up   typical 10-25% as directed by the manufacturer  NOW LET IT BE RESOLVED ,   by the Council of the Township of West Orange   that the   Fire  Apparatus maintenance and   r epair contract be   awarded to   Fire & Safety Services for the term of 24  mont hs with the option to extend one (1) additional year   which will start 01/01/2023 .  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that the   Acting   Purchasing Agent   is hereby authorized to arrange to pay  for the forgoing in accordance with the terms of   the purchase order   a nd from funds certified by the Chief  Financial Officer.  Karen   J.   Carnevale,   R.M.C.,   Municipal Cle rk   Susan McCartney ,   Council President  Adopted: December 20, 2022  I hereby certify funds available :   01-2010-00-7902-153  John O. Gross,   CFO /BA

documents.title: AUTHORIZING SECOND LICENSE AMENDMENT TO LEASE AGREEMENT WITH NJ TRANSIT FOR USE MISSISSIPPI AVENUE LOOP PROPERTY - 2022

pages.text: 321- 22  December 20, 2022  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   or   about   September   1 5 ,   2012 ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the  “ Township ” )   entered   into   a   License   Agreement ,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ”   with   the   New  Jersey   Transit   Corporation   ( “ NJ   Transit ” )   for   the   use   of   a   20,25 0   square   foot   ar ea   comprising   a  portion   of   the   Mississippi   Avenue   Loop,   situated   on   the   easterly   side   of   Mississippi   Avenue,  north of Harrison Avenue (the   “ Property ” ) within the Township; and  WHEREAS,   in   2017,   the   License   Agreement   was   previ ously   amended   to   extend   the  t erm of the license   for an additional five (5 ) y ear period ,   annexe d hereto as Exhibit   “ B ; ”   and  WHEREAS   the   latest term of the l icense expired on July 31, 20 22 ; and  WHEREAS,   t he   Township   and   NJ   Transit   have   agreed   to   amend   the   License   to   extend  the   use   of   the   Property   until   July   31,   202 7,   pursuant   to   the   terms   and   conditions   of   a   Second  License Amendment, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ C ”   (the   “ Amendment ” ); and  WHEREAS   the   Amendme nt   provides   that   the   Township   shall   continue   to   use   the  Property and pay an Annua l Fee as set forth in paragraph 2 of the Amendment.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   that   the   Mayor   shall   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to  execute   the   Amendment   annexed   hereto   as   Exh ibit   “ C ” ,   and   the   Mu nicipal   Clerk   shall   be   and  hereby is authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature; and

BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED ,   that   the   original   fully   executed   cop y   of   this   Resolution  and   the   executed   Amendment   to   the   Lease   with   the   initial   executed   copy   of   the   original   L icense  between the Township and   NJ Transit .  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council Preside nt  Adopted:   December 20, 2022  I hereby certify fun ds available from   Account No.   01-2010-00-8052-090  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer  4852-9937-2672, v. 1
Exhibit   “A”

License   #L0775-3542-01 LICENSE   AGREEMENT  THIS   LICENSE   AGREEMENT   is   made   on   this Transit   Corporation,   an   instrumentality   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey 1   ,   with   an   office   at   One   Penn   Plaza   East, Newark,   New   Jersey   07105-2246,   (hereinafter   referred   to   as   ("NJ   TRANSIT")   and   Township   of   West Orange,   a   municipal   corporation,   with   an   office   located   at   66   Main   Street,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey 07052   (hereinafter   "Licensee"). day   of   2012,   between   New   Jersey WHEREAS,   NJ   TRANSIT   owns   the   property   described   as   a   rectangular   parcel   of   land   containing approximately   20,250   (150 ’   x   135 ’ )   square   feet,   comprising   a   portion   of   the   Mississippi   Avenue   Loop, situated   on   the   easterly   side   of   Mississippi   Avenue,   north   of   Harrison   Avenue,   located   in   the   Township   of West   Orange,   County   of   Essex,   State   of   New   Jersey   as   shown   on   Exhibit   A,   attached   hereto   and   made   a part   hereof   (hereinafter   the   “ License   Area ” );   and WHEREAS,   the   parties   have   agreed   that   NJ   TRANSIT   will   grant   unto   Licensee   permission   to   use   the License   Area,   pursuant   to   the   terms   and   conditions   as   herein   set   forth. NOW,   THEREFORE,   in   consideration   of   the   mutual   covenants,   terms   and   conditions   set   forth   in   this agreement,   and   for   other   good   and   valuable   consideration,   the   parties   agree   as   follows:  1.   Term   of   License  NJ   TRANSIT   grants   Licensee   permission   to   enter   upon   and   continue   to   use   the   “License   Area ” for   the   purposes   stated   herein   for   a   term   of   five   (5)   years   commencing   on   August   1,   2012   (the "Commencement   Date")   and   ending   on   July   31,   2017   (the   "Termination   Date").   NJ   TRANSIT may   cancel   this   License   in   its   entirety   or   for   any   licensed   location   without   cause   and   for convenience   upon   thirty   (30)   days   written   notice   to   Licensee.  2.   Location   of   License   Area  Licensee   shall   not   use   the   space   adjacent   to   the   License   Area   or   any   other   space   not   specifically assigned   to   Licensee.   There   may   be   no   physical   contact   with   any   NJ   TRANSIT   equipment   by Licensee.   NJ   TRANSIT   reserves   the   right   to   change   the   location   of   the   License   Area   to   meet   the needs   of   NJ   TRANSIT. In   the   event   that   during   the   term   of   this   License,   NJ   TRANSIT   undertakes   activities   at   the premises   which   may   involve   hazardous   or   offensive   activities,   including   the   placement   of construction   facilities,   equipment   and   materials   in   the   immediate   vicinity   of   the   License   Area, Licensee   agrees   that   neither   NJ   TRANSIT   nor   its   contractor   shall   be   held   liable   for   any annoyance,   disturbance   or   loss   of   business   due   to   these   conditions.  3.   Use The   License   Area   shall   be   used   solely   for   the   purpose   of   providing   the   Township   with   metered automobile   parking   and   for   no   other   purpose.   Licensee   shall   not   use   and   shall   not   allow,   permit or   suffer   the   License   Area,   or   any   part   thereof,   to   be   used   for   any   other   purpose   without   the   prior written   consent   of   NJ   TRANSIT,   which   may   be   granted   or   denied   in   its   sole   and   absolute discretion.

4.   Fee The   Fee   for   the   License   Area   is   as   follows:   (all   compensation   is   payable   annually) License   Year   Annual   Minimum   Base   Fee $2,121.75 $2,174.79 $2,229.16 $2,284.89 $2,342.01 1  2 4 5  Charges   are   due   and   payable   on   the   first   day   of   each   License   Year   in   advance,   commencing   on August   1,   2012.   All   checks   shall   be   made   payable   to   NJ   TRANSIT   Corporation   and   shall   be mailed   to   Post   Office   Box   1549,   Newark,   New   Jersey   07101-1549.  5.   Utilities Licensee   shall   pay   for   any   and   all   electric   services,   water   and   sewerage   charges   necessary   for Licensee's   use.   NJ   TRANSIT   shall   not   be   liable   for   any   temporary   suspension   of   any   utilities. 6.   Services  Licensee   acknowledges   that   NJ   TRANSIT   is   not   providing   any   services   for   the   License   Area.  7.   Maintenance,   Repairs   and   Security  Licensee,   at   Licensee's   sole   cost,   shall   maintain   the   License   Area   in   constant   good   order,   repair and   safe   condition   and   shall   make   all   repairs   to   the   License   Area   which   is   necessary   or   desirable to   keep   the   License   Area   in   good   order   and   repair,   including   but   not   limited   to   maintaining   the fence   that   surrounds   the   License   Area,   snow   removal,   de-icing   and   surface   repair.   Licensee   shall keep   the   License   Area   free   from   any   nuisance,   including   but   not   limited   to   vermin,   escaping offensive   odors   and   hazardous   conditions.  8.   Alterations,   Additions   or   Improvements  Licensee   shall,   at   its   sole   cost,   make   all   alterations   and   improvements   to   the   License   Area   as   are necessary   to   permit   the   use   of   the   License   Area.   Notwithstanding   the   above,   Licensee   shall   not make   any   alterations,   additions   or   improvements   on   or   to   the   License   Area   without   the   prior written   consent   of   NJ   TRANSIT.  Compliance   with   Laws  Licensee   shall   comply   with   all   the   laws,   ordinances,   regulations   and   rules   of   the   federal,   state   and municipal   governments   which   may   be   applicable   to   the   use   of   the   License   Area,   and   shall,   at   its sole   cost   and   expense,   obtain   such   licenses,   approvals   or   authority   from   federal,   state   or municipal   governments   or   agencies   thereof,   as   are   necessary   to   use   the   License   Area.   The Licensee   shall   also   pay   any   and   all   federal,   state,   municipal   or   other   fees,   assessments   or   taxes imposed   or   levied   upon   such   operations   and   shall   defend   and   save   NJ   TRANSIT   harmless   from any   and   all   fees,   assessments,   taxes,   fines   or   penalties   arising   in   connection   with   such   operations and/or   any   and   all   activities   conducted   on   NJ   TRANSIT   premises   by   Licensee.   The   provisions   of this   License   and   all   rights   and   obligations   herein   shall   be   governed   by   and   construed   in accordance   with   the   laws   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey. 9.  10.   Environmental   Regulations   and   Indemnity Licensee   shall   maintain   and   keep,   at   its   sole   cost,   the   License   Area   thereon   in   compliance   with   all applicable   federal,   state   and   local   environmental   laws,   rules   and   regulations   including,   but   not  2

limited   to,   the   New   Jersey   Spill   Compensation   and   Control   Act,   N.J.S.A.   58:10-23.1   1   et   seq.   (the “ Spill   Act ” )   the   Industrial   Site   Recoveiy   Act,   N.J.S.A.   13:lK-6   et   seq.   ( “ ISRA ” )   and   the Underground   Storage   of   Hazardous   Substances   Act,   N.J.S.A.   58:10A-21   et   seq.;   Licensee   shall also   comply   with   all   registration   and   disclosure   provisions   of   laws   and   regulations   designed   to prevent,   control   or   respond   to   the   discharge   of   hazardous   substances   into   the   land,   water   and   air. Licensee   shall   be   responsible   for   all   remedial,   investigatory   and   corrective   measures   and   other such   actions   as   may   be   required   in   connection   with   such   laws,   rules   and   regulations   resulting from   or   during   Licensee ’ s   use   and   occupancy   of   the   License   Area. 11.   Obligations   of   NJ   TRANSIT NJ   TRANSIT   shall   assume   no   obligation   whatsoever   in   connection   with   the   operation   of   the License   Area   or   the   use   of   the   License   Area   by   Licensee,   including,   but   not   limited   to   the provision   of   security   or   lighting,   and   shall   not   be   obligated   to   make   any   repairs   to   the   License Area   or   to   furnish   workers,   equipment   or   materials   in   connection   with   such   operation   or   use.   NJ TRANSIT   shall   not   be   liable   to   Licensee   for   loss,   damage   or   liability   of   any   kind   or   nature whatsoever   sustained   by   Licensee,   its   successors   or   assigns   by   reason   of   NJ   TRANSIT'S   failure   to fulfill   its   obligations   herein   or   by   reason   of   any   event   of   any   kind   beyond   the   control   of   NJ TRANSIT   which   in   any   way   affects   NJ   TRANSIT'S   ability   to   perform   its   obligations   herein. Furthermore,   NJ   TRANSIT   shall   not   be   liable   for   any   loss,   theft   or   damage   whatsoever   to   the License   Area   or   its   contents   or   for   any   loss   of   business   which   may   be   caused   by   the   actions   or inactions   of   NJ   TRANSIT. 12.   Obligations   of   Licensee Licensee   shall   take   all   measures   to   assure   the   safety   of   the   License   Area,   including   but   not   limited to   all   measures   required   by   applicable   law. 13.   Indemnity Licensee,   at   its   sole   cost   and   expense,   shall   indemnify,   defend   and   save   harmless   the   State   of New   Jersey,   NJ   TRANSIT   and   its   operating   divisions,   their   agents,   officials,   employees,   servants, invitees   and   any   other   operating   authorities   NJ   TRANSIT   may   designate,   (the   "Indemnified Parties")   against   all   claims,   just   or   unjust,   made   against   the   Indemnified   Parties   or   Licensee   on account   of   injuries,   death,   or   losses   of   any   kind   whatsoever,   including   but   not   limited   to   property losses,   damages,   suits,   liabilities,   judgments,   costs   and   expenses   which   may   in   anyway   accrue against   the   Indemnified   Parties   (other   than   due   to   the   gross   negligence   of   NJ   TRANSIT),   in consequence   of   the   granting   of   this   License,   the   failure   of   Licensee   to   perform   any   of   its obligations   under   this   License,   and/or   the   use   or   operation   of   the   License   Area   hereunder   or which   may   in   any   way   result   therefrom,   and   whether   or   not   it   shall   be   alleged   or   determined   that the   cause   thereof   was   the   negligence,   acts   or   omissions   of   the   Indemnified   Parties   or   Licensee   or of   other   persons.   Licensee   shall   appear,   defend   and   pay,   at   its   own   expense,   all   costs,   including counsel   fees,   arising   therefrom   or   incurred   in   connection   therewith,   and   if   any   judgment   shall   be rendered   against   the   Indemnified   Parties   in   any   such   action,   Licensee   shall,   at   its   own   expense, satisfy   and   discharge   the   same. 14.   Insurance Licensee,   at   its   expense,   shall   keep   in   force   during   the   term   of   this   agreement   (a)   Worker ’ s Compensation   and   Employer's   Liability   Insurance,   if   required   by   law   and   (b)   Comprehensive General   Liability   Insurance   to   include   personal   injury,   bodily   injury,   broad   form   property damage,   operations   hazard   owner's   protective   coverage,   contractual   liability,   products   and completed   operations   liability   in   combined   single   limit   of   not   less   than   Five   Million   Dollars ($5,000,000.00).   The   minimum   limits   specified   above   are   the   minimum   amounts   required   by   NJ TRANSIT,   and   amounts   may   be   revised   by   NJ   TRANSIT   from   time   to   time   to   meet   changed

circumstances.   Licensee   shall   provide   NJ   TRANSIT   with   a   certificate   of   insurance   evidencing   the aforesaid   insurance   coverage   upon   execution   of   this   License.   NEW   JERSEY   TRANSIT   AND THE   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY   SHALL   BE   NAMED   ADDITIONAL   INSUREDS   ON   ALL INSURANCE   POLICIES   REQUIRED   HEREUNDER.   The   insurance   policies   shall   provide   that there   shall   be   no   cancellation,   change   or   lapse   in   coverage   without   at   least   thirty   (30)   days   prior written   notice   to   NJ   TRANSIT.   It   is   understood   and   agreed   that   the   procurement   of   insurance   in the   amounts   required   does   not   in   any   way   or   manner   whatsoever   limit   Licensee's   liability   to   NJ TRANSIT.   In   the   event   the   insurance   so   provided   by   Licensee   does   not   apply   to   or   cover   a particular   loss,   Licensee   shall   be   liable   to   NJ   TRANSIT   for   the   full   amount   of   any   and   all   loss and/or   damage   as   provided   herein.   Licensee   shall   also   be   liable   to   NJ   TRANSIT   for   the   full amount   of   any   and   all   loss   and/or   damage   for   which   Licensee   is   responsible. 15.   Termination   bv   Default If   Licensee   defaults   on   any   term   or   condition   set   forth   herein,   including   failure   to   pay compensation   for   any   or   all   of   the   locations   as   described   herein,   and   if   such   default   shall   not   have been   cured   within   five   (5)   days   of   written   notice   by   NJ   TRANSIT,   NJ   TRANSIT   may   by   written notice   to   Licensee   immediately   terminate   this   License. 16.   Cross   Default Licensee   agrees   that   a   default   by   a   licensee   which   is   an   ‘   Affiliate ”   of   Licensee   under   any   license with   NJ   TRANSIT   shall   be   deemed   to   be   a   default   under   this   License.   An   Affiliate   shall   mean   a person   or   entity   which   is   controlled   by,   controlling,   or   under   common   control   with   Licensee. Assignment This   License   may   not   be   assigned   or   transferred   without   the   prior   written   consent   of   NJ TRANSIT.   Notwithstanding   the   foregoing,   Licensee   may   assign   this   agreement   to   an   entity   (i)   for which   the   majority   of   the   ownership   interests   are   owned   or   purchased   by   Licensee,   (ii)   which purchases   or   owns   the   majority   of   the   ownership   interests   of   Licensee,   or   (iii)   which   results   from the   merger   of   Licensee,   without   the   consent   of,   but   upon   notice   to   the   NJ   TRANSIT.   However, the   assignee   shall   assume   the   liabilities   and   responsibilities   under   this   License   with   the   assignor simultaneously   retaining   same. 17. 18.   Notice All   notices   required   by   this   License   shall   be   sent   by   certified   .mail,   return   receipt   requested,   to   the parties   at   the   addresses   listed   below,   unless   either   party   shall   inform   the   other   party,   in   writing,   of any   change   in   designated   parties   or   addresses. NJ   TRANSIT:   Sr.   Director   of   Real   Estate New   Jersey   Transit   Corporation One   Penn   Plaza   East Newark,   New   Jersey   07105-2246 Licensee:   Township   of   West   Orange 66   Main   Street West   Orange,   New   Jersey   07052 Attn:   Municipal   Clerk 19.   Non-Waiver   bv   NJ   TRANSIT The   various   rights,   remedies,   options   and   elections   of   NJ   TRANSIT   expressed   herein   are  4

cumulative,   and   the   failure   of   NJ   TRANSIT   to   enforce   strict   performance   by   Licensee   of   the conditions   and   covenants   of   this   License   or   to   exercise   any   election   or   option   or   to   restore   or   have recourse   to   any   remedy   herein   conferred   or   the   acceptance   by   NJ   TRANSIT   of   any   installment   of rent   after   breach   by   Licensee,   in   any   one   or   more   instances,   shall   not   be   construed   or   deemed   to be   a   waiver   or   a   relinquishment   for   the   future   enforcement   by   NJ   TRANSIT   of   any   such conditions   and   covenants,   options,   elections   or   remedies,   but   the   same   shall   continue   in   full   force and   effect.  20.   Right   of   Access  NJ   TRANSIT   shall   allow   Licensee,   its   employees,   agents   and   customers   necessary   and reasonable   means   of   ingress   and   egress   through   NJ   TRANSIT   premises   for   the   purpose   of gaining   access   to   the   License   Area.  21.   Entire   Agreement  This   License   embodies   the   entire   agreement   between   the   parties.   It   may   not   be   modified   or terminated   except   as   provided   herein.   If   any   provision   is   held   to   be   invalid,   it   shall   be   considered deleted   herefrom   and   shall   not   invalidate   the   remaining   provisions.  22.   Modification   of   Agreement  No   term   of   the   License   may   be   changed   without   the   prior   written   consent   of   both   parties.  23.   Authority   to   Sign  Licensee   represents   that   the   individual   executing   this   License   on   behalf   of   Licensee   is   fully authorized   to   do   so   and   that   the   execution   of   this   License   on   the   part   of   such   individual   shall   bind Licensee   to   its   obligations   set   forth   in   this   License. L0775-3542-01 FIRST   LICENSE   AMENDMENT BETWEEN NEW   JERSEY   TRANSIT   CORPORATION AND TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE  This   License   Amendment   (hereinafter   “ Amendment ” )   made   this 2018,   by   and   between   New   Jersey   Transit   Corporation,   an   instrumentality   of   the   State   of   New Jersey,   whose   address   is   One   Penn   Plaza   East,   Newark,   New   Jersey   07105-2246   (hereinafter   “ NJ TRANSIT ” )   and   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   a   municipal   corporation,   with   an   office   located at   66   Main   Street,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey   07052   (hereinafter   “ Licensee ” ). day   of WHEREAS,   NJ   TRANSIT   and   Licensee   originally   entered   into   a   License   Agreement (hereinafter   “ License ” )   on   September   15,   2012,   for   a   rectangular   parcel   of   land   containing approximately   20,250   (150 ’   x   135   ’ )   square   feet,   comprising   a   portion   of   the   Mississippi   Avenue Loop,   situated   on   the   easterly   side   of   Mississippi   Avenue,   north   of   Harrison   Avenue,   located   in the   Township   of   West   Orange,   County   of   Essex,   State   of   New   Jersey   (hereinafter,   the   “ License Area   ”);   and WHEREAS,   the   original   Term   of   the   License   expired   on   July   31,   2017;   and WHEREAS,   NJ   TRANSIT   and   Licensee   have   agreed   to   extend   the   License   for   a   five   year   (5) term   with   a   revised   Expiration   Date   of   July   31,   2022;   and WHEREAS,   NJ   TRANSIT   and   Licensee   have   agreed   to   amend   the   License   to   provide   for revised   Annual   Fee   in   the   amount   of   $2,401.00   per   year,   with   annual   increases   in   the   amount   of two   and   one-half   percent   (2.5%),   to   be   payable   by   Licensee,   in   addition   to   all   other   forms   of   Fee and/or   Additional   Fee   as   provided   by   the   License;   and WHEREAS,   NJ   TRANSIT   and   Licensee   have   agreed   to   such   amendment   of   the   License   subject to   the   tenns   and   conditions   of   this   Amendment. NOW,   THEREFORE,   in   consideration   of   the   mutual   covenants,   terms   and   conditions   set   forth herein,   the   Parties   hereto   agree   as   follows: 1.   Effective   August   1,   2017,   the   License   Term,   as   amended,   shall   commence   on   August   1, 2017,   and   shall   expire   on   July   31,   2022,   unless   sooner   tenninated   as   provided   in   the License. 2.   Effective   August   1,   2017,   Section   4   is   deleted   in   its   entirety   and   replaced   by   the following:

The   Fee   for   the   License   Area   is   as   follows:   (The   Fee   is   payable   annually) License   Y   ear 1   (8/1/17-7/31/18) 2(8/1/18-7/31/19) 3   (8/1/19-7/31/20) 4(8/1/20-7/31/21) 5(8/1/21-7/31/22) Annual   Fee $2,401.00 $2,461.00 $2,523.00 $2,586.00 $2,651.00 Charges   are   due   and   payable   on   the   first   day   of   each   License   Year   in   advance, commencing   on   August   1,   2017.   All   checks   shall   be   made   payable   to   NJ   TRANSIT Corporation   and   shall   be   mailed   to   Post   Office   Box   1549,   Newark,   New   Jersey   07101- 1549. 3.   Effective   August   1,   2017,   Exhibit   A   of   the   License   shall   be   replaced   with   the   Exhibit   A attached   hereto. 4.   All   other   terms   and   conditions   of   said   License   shall   remain   in   full   force   and   effect.   In   the event   of   an   inconsistency   between   this   Amendment   and   the   License,   the   terms   of   this Amendment   shall   control. 5.   This   Amendment   is   hereby   made   a   part   of   the   License. Agreed   and   accepted   this   day   of   ,2018.  NEW   JERSEY   TRANSIT   CORPORATION   WITNESS  By: Jeffrey   A.   Nad   ell Senior   Director   -   Real   Estate   and   Economic Development  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   WITNESS  By: Name: Title: This   License   Amendment   has   been   approved   as   to   fonn   only. L0775-3542-01  SECOND LICENSE AMENDMENT  BETWEEN  NEW JERSEY TRANSIT CORPORATION  AND  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ______________________________________________  This License Amendment (hereinafter “Amendment”) is an agreement between New Jersey  Transit Corporation, an instrumentality of the State of New Jersey, whose address is One Penn  Plaza East, Newark, New Jersey 07105-2246 (hereinafter “NJ TRANSIT”) and the Township of  West Orange, a municipal corporation, with an office at 66 Main Street, West Orange, New Jersey  07052 (hereinafter “Licensee”).  WHEREAS, NJ TRANSIT and Licensee originally entered into a License Agreement (hereinafter  “License”) on September 15, 2012, for a rectangular parcel of land containing approximately  20,250 (150’ x 135’) square feet, comprising a portion of the Mississippi Avenue Loop, situated  on the easterly side of Mississippi Avenue, north of Harrison Avenue, located in the Township of  West Orange, County of Essex, State of New Jersey (hereinafter, the “License Area”); and  WHEREAS, NJ TRANSIT and Licensee entered into the First License Amendment on January 8,  2019, which, inter alia, extended the Term of the License to July 31, 2022; and  WHEREAS, the Term of the First License Amendment expired on July 31, 2022; and  WHEREAS, NJ TRANSIT and Licensee have agreed to extend the License for an additional five  year (5) term with a revised Expiration Date of July 31, 2027; and  WHEREAS, NJ TRANSIT and Licensee have agreed to amend the License to provide for revised  Annual Fee in the amount of $2,717.28 per year, with annual increases in the amount of two and  one-half percent (2.5%), to be payable by Licensee, in addition to all other forms of Fee and/or  Additional Fee as provided by the License; and  WHEREAS, NJ TRANSIT and Licensee have agreed to such amendment of the License subject  to the terms and conditions of this Amendment.  NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants, terms and conditions set forth  herein, the Parties hereto agree as follows:

2  1.   Effective August 1, 2022, the License Term, as amended, shall commence on August 1,  2022, and shall expire on July 31, 2027, unless sooner terminated as provided in the  License.  2.   Effective August 1, 2022, Section 4 is deleted in its entirety and replaced by the following:  4. Fee  The Fee for the License Area is as follows: (The Fee is payable annually)  License Year   Annual Fee  1 (8/1/22-7/31/23)   $2,717.28  2 (8/1/23-7/31/24)   $2,785.21  3 (8/1/24-7/31/25)   $2,854.84  4 (8/1/25-7/31/26)   $2,926.21  5 (8/1/26-7/31/27)   $2,999.36  Fees and any other charges are due and payable on the first day of each License Year in  advance, commencing on August 1, 2022. All checks shall be made payable to NJ  TRANSIT Corporation and shall be mailed to Post Office Box P.O. Box 416168, Boston,  Massachusetts 02241-6168.  3.   Effective August 1, 2022, Section 13 is deleted in its entirety and replaced by the following:  13. Indemnity  To the fullest extent permitted by law, Licensee, and any of its contracting parties, at its  sole cost and expense, shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless the State of New Jersey,  NJ TRANSIT and its operating divisions, their agents, officials, employees, servants,  invitees   and   any   other   operating   authorities   NJ   TRANSIT   may   designate,   (the  "Indemnified Parties") from and against all claims and losses, just or unjust,   made against  the Indemnified Parties on account of injuries, death, occupational disease of any person,  including any of Licensee’s or its contractor’s employees or agents, or losses of any kind  whatsoever, including, but not limited to, property losses, damages, personal injuries,  death, suits, liabilities, judgments, costs and expenses which may in any way accrue against  the Indemnified Parties (other than due to   the willful misconduct of NJ TRANSIT or any  Indemnified Party), in consequence of the granting of this License, the failure of Licensee  to perform any of its obligations under this License, and/or the use or operation of the  License Area hereunder or which may in any way result therefrom, and whether or not it  shall be alleged or determined that the cause thereof was the negligence, acts or omissions  of the Indemnified Parties or Licensee or of other persons. Licensee shall appear, defend  and pay, at its own expense, all costs, including counsel fees, arising therefrom or incurred  in connection therewith, and if any judgment shall be rendered against the Indemnified  Parties in any such action, Licensee shall, at its own expense, satisfy and discharge the  same.  In defense of any such claims, demands, suits, actions or proceedings, the Licensee shall  not raise or introduce without the express written permission in advance of the Attorney  General of the State of New Jersey, any defense involving in any way the immunity of NJ

3  Transit or the State of New Jersey, the jurisdiction of the tribunal over NJ Transit or the  State of New Jersey, or the provisions of any statue respecting suits against NJ Transit or  the State of New Jersey.  4.   Effective August 1, 2022, Section 14 is deleted in its entirety and replaced by the following:  14. Insurance  (a)   Licensee shall procure and maintain, and shall require its assignees and contractor(s),  if any, to procure and maintain, during the entire term, and any holdover period,  insurance against claims for injuries to persons or damages to property which may arise  from or in connection with the Licensee’s operation and use of the licensed premises.  The cost of such insurance shall be borne by the Licensee.  MINIMUM SCOPE AND LIMIT OF INSURANCE  Coverage shall be at least as broad as of the following kinds and minimum amounts:  1. Commercial General Liability (CGL): Insurance Services Office Form CG 00 01  covering CGL on an “occurrence” basis, including products and completed operations,  property damage, bodily injury and personal & advertising injury with limits no less  than $5,000,000 per occurrence. If a general aggregate limit applies, either the general  aggregate limit shall apply separately to this project/location (ISO CG 25 03 or 25 04)  or the general aggregate limit shall be twice the required occurrence limit. Coverage  provided under this liability policy shall include premises operations liability, blanket  contractual liability, independent contractor’s liability, mobile equipment, damage  from explosion, collapse and underground hazards, and cross liability and severability  of interests clause. There shall be no coverage exceptions for property containing or  adjacent to railroad facilities or other transportation facilities and the policies shall be  endorsed evidencing the contractual exclusion related to construction activity, or any  access whatsoever, within fifty (50) feet of the railroad has been deleted.  2. Workers’ Compensation and Employer’s Liability Insurance: Licensee shall and  shall require its assignees and/or contractor(s), if any, to purchase and maintain during  the term of this License, a policy of worker’s compensation insurance within the  statutory limits of the State of New Jersey. In case any class of employees is not  protected under the Worker’s Compensation Statute, Licensee shall provide and shall  cause each contractor to provide employer’s liability insurance for the protection of  each of its employees as are not otherwise protected. Such policy shall cover any  disease, including communicable disease, occupational and bacterial, and, if permitted  by law, shall contain a waiver of subrogation in the benefit of the Indemnified parties.  Employer’s Liability Insurance with limits of no less than:  Employer’s Liability   $1,000,000 each accident  $1,000,000 each employee disease  $1,000,000 policy limit – disease  3. If the Licensed Parcel is within 50 feet of any rail, Railroad Protective Liability  Insurance: Where work performed pursuant to Sections 7 or 8 includes work across,

4  under or within 50 feet of railroad tracks or the right-of-way, Lessee shall or shall  cause the Contractor to procure and maintain Railroad Protective Liability Insurance  (AAR – AASHTO Form) in the name of NJ TRANSIT Rail Operations and other  affected railroads providing for bodily injury limits of not less than $2,000,000 per  occurrence for damage arising out of bodily injury or death, and railroad protection  property damage limits of not less than $2,000,000 for damages to or destruction of  property, including the loss of use thereof, in any one occurrence, and not less than  $6,000,000 In the aggregate. (Reference: “Standard Provisions for General Liability  Policies” as contained in U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Highway  Administration, Federal-Aid Highway Program Manual Volume 6, Chapter 6,  Section 2, Sub-Section 2, Attachment 1).  The Railroad Protection Policy must contain the following endorsements:  a.   I.S.O. (Insurance Services) Office Endorsement: GL 00 30 03 83.  b.   Manuscript endorsement with the following wording: “It is understood and agreed  that Insuring Agreements, Section II, Definitions (c) (3) is amended to read: ‘Any  employee of the insured not within (1) or (2) who is specifically loaned or assigned  to the work of the Contractor for prevention of accidents or protection of property,  regardless at whose cost those services are provided.’  c.   The Licensee shall furnish to Licensor and other specified railroads a Certificate  of Insurance for Contractor’s Public Liability Insurance and the original of the  AAR-AASHTO policy for Railroad Protective Liability Insurance and all other  insurance as required, prior to Execution of the Agreement.  d.   Each policy shall include specific endorsements to said policy or policies as stated  in this Article providing for thirty (30) days written notice to Licensor of any  cancellation or material change in the policy.  If any loss is paid to Licensee, it will be held by Licensee in trust for application to the  cost of restoring, repairing, replacing or rebuilding the Licensed Parcel, and will be  disbursed promptly upon receipt by Licensee.  If the Licensee, or any of its Contractors, maintains broader coverage or policies,  and/or higher limits than the minimum shown above, then the full limits of that  insurance coverages or policies will be available to respond to any claims asserted  against the Licensor or the Indemnified Parties.  The foregoing insurance coverage is not intended to, nor does it limit the liability of  the Licensee to hold the Indemnified Parties harmless as set forth in Section 13 above.  (b)   Other Insurance Provisions: The insurance policies are to contain, or be endorsed to  contain, the following provisions:  1.   Additional Insured Status: NJ TRANSIT, the State of New Jersey, other railroad(s)  operating on the affected property, the State of New Jersey, their successors, assigns,  agents, employees, servants, and officials, and each and every one of the Indemnified  Parties, are to be covered as additional insureds on the CGL policy with respect to

5  liability arising out of Licensed premises or any work or operations performed by or on  behalf of the Licensee including materials, parts, or equipment furnished in connection  with such work or operations. General liability coverage can be provided in the form  of an endorsement to the Licensee’s insurance (at least as broad as ISO Form CG 20  10). Additional insured endorsement CG2026 11/85, CG 2010 11/85 or CG 2010 10/93  (but only if modified to include both ongoing and completed operations) naming the  Indemnified Parties.  2.   Primary Coverage: For any claims related to this contract, the Licensee’s insurance  coverage shall be primary insurance coverage at least as broad as ISO CG 20 01 04 13  as respects to the Licensor, its officers, officials, employees, volunteers, and any  indemnified party. Any insurance or self-insurance maintained by the Licensor, its  officers, officials, employees, or volunteers shall not contribute to any loss or claim.  3.   Notice of Cancellation: Each insurance policy required above shall provide that  coverage shall not be canceled, and shall be endorsed stating the following cancellation  notice:  “This policy is not subject to cancellation or material change until thirty (30) days  after NJ TRANSIT has received written notice thereof as evidenced by return  receipt   of   a   registered   letter   addressed   to   NJ   TRANSIT,   Attn:   Greystone  Management Solutions, One Penn Plaza East, Real Estate Department, 7th Floor,  Newark, NJ 07105-2246.”  4.   Waiver of Subrogation: Licensee hereby grants to Licensor a waiver of any right to  subrogation which any insurer of said Licensee may acquire against the Licensor by  virtue of the payment of any loss under such insurance. Licensee agrees to obtain any  endorsement that may be necessary to affect this waiver of subrogation, but this  provision applies regardless of whether or not the Licensor has received a waiver of  subrogation endorsement from the insurer.  5.   Acceptability of Insurers: Insurance is to be placed with insurers authorized to  conduct business in the state of New Jersey with a current A.M. Best’s rating of no  less than A: VIII.  6.   Self-Insured Retentions: Self-insured retentions must be declared to and approved by  the Licensor. At the option of the Licensor, either, the Licensee shall obtain coverage  to reduce or eliminate such self-insured retentions; or the Licensee shall provide a  financial guarantee satisfactory to the Licensor, guaranteeing payment of losses and  related investigations, claim administration, and defense expenses. If any such program  is approved by Licensor, the Licensee and any of its Contractors represent that such  program provides the Indemnified Parties with all rights, immunities and protections  that would be provided by traditional independent insurance required under the  License, including, but not limited to, the defense obligations that insurers are required  to undertake in the liability policies pursuant to the terms of the License.  7.   Verification of Coverage: Licensee, and any of its contractors, shall furnish the  Licensor with original Certificates of Insurance including all required amendatory  endorsements and a copy of the Declarations and Endorsement Page of the CGL policy  listing all policy endorsements to Licensor prior to occupancy or commencement of  any work of Improvements, Alterations or “Major Repairs” as outlined in this

6  Agreement. However, failure to obtain the required documents prior to occupancy or  commencement of any work or Improvements shall not waive the Licensee’s obligation  to provide them. The Licensor reserves the right to require complete, certified copies  of   all   required   insurance   policies,   including   endorsements,   required   by   these  specifications, at any time. Said certificates and endorsements shall be delivered to NJ  TRANSIT, Attn: Greystone Management Solutions, One Penn Plaza East, Real Estate  Department, 7th Floor, Newark, NJ 07105-2246. The Certificate of Insurance must  state in the description of operations section the License number.  8.   Special   Risks   or   Circumstances:   Licensor   reserves   the   right   to   modify   these  requirements at any time, including limits, based on the nature of the risk, prior  experience, insurer, coverage, or other special circumstances, and is customarily  maintained for buildings of similar construction, use and class in the area in which the  License Area is located. Each insurance policy required to be maintained under this  License shall state that with respect to the interest of Licensor and the Additional  Insureds, the insurance maintained pursuant to each such policy shall not be invalidated  by any action or inaction of Licensee and shall insure Licensor and the Additional  Insureds   regardless   of   any breach or   violation   of   any   warranties,   declarations,  conditions or exclusions by Licensee. It is understood and agreed that the procurement  of insurance in the amounts required does not in any way or manner whatsoever limit  Licensee's liability to NJ TRANSIT. In the event the insurance so provided by Licensee  does not apply to or cover a particular loss, Licensee shall be liable to NJ TRANSIT  for the full amount of any and all loss and/or damage as provided herein.   Licensee  shall also be liable to NJ TRANSIT for the full amount of any and all loss and/or  damage for which Licensee is responsible.  5.   All other terms and conditions of said License shall remain in full force and effect. In the  event of an inconsistency between this Amendment and the License, the terms of this  Amendment shall control.  6.   Authority to Sign  Licensee represents that the individual executing this Second License Amendment to the  License on behalf of the Licensee is fully authorized to do so and that the execution of  this Second Amendment on the part of such individual shall bind Licensee to its  obligations set forth in Second Amendment.  7.   This Amendment is hereby made a part of the License.

pages.text: 322-22  December 20, 2022  Resolution to Authorize the Purchase of   furniture   and   Equipment   through the Education Services  Commission of NJ (ESCNJ)   Cooperative Contracts # ESCNJ 2 2/23-08   Co-op # 65MCESCCPS   and  NJ State Contract A-81631, A-81711, A-81718  WHEREAS,   Township of West Orange   has obtained a new location for the West Orange Public  Library and there is a need to purchase new furniture   and equipment ; and  WHEREAS,   the Township has been the success ful recipient of Library Bond Grant Funds from the  State of New Jersey; and  WHEREAS,   the Library Board and its Executive Director have been working closely with the  Township concerning the New Library ( “ New Library Project ” ); and  WHEREAS,   pursuant to th e provision made in the Local Public Contracts Law N.J.S.A. 40A:11-5(2)   the  Township as a Local Government Unit, may without public advertising for goods or services enter  into contracts already secured by the United States of America, the State of New Jer sey, County or  Municipality; and  WHEREAS ,   the Township as a member of the Educational Services Commission of NJ (ESCNJ)  cooperative can utilize the cooperative contracts with the following Compan y   see Exhibit   “ A ”  annexed hereto for cost   detail   NJ State Approved Co-op No. 65MCESCCPS  Bid Number ESCNJ 22/23-08 :  Mediatechnologies NJ, LLC  892 Industrial Park Drive  Shelby, Michigan 49455  Total cost of furniture- $85,017.13  Nickerson Corp  I/C LII  515 Union Ave  Union Beach, FL 07735  Total cost of furniture- $123,044.99  Authorized Dealer for Estey  Library Interiors Inc  9 9 0 Cedarbridge Ave  PMB # 329  Brick, NJ 08723  Total cost of   Shelving -   $230,104.96  Krueger International, Inc  1130 Bellevue St  P.O. Box 8100  Green Bay, WI 54308-8100  Total cost of furniture-   $8,446.78  The State Division of Purchase and Property has awarded contracts to the following vendors see  Exhibit   “ B ”   annexed hereto for cost detail   NJ State Contract A-81718, A-81711, and A-81631:  JSI c/o Library Interiors I nc  225 Clay St  Jasper, Indiana 47546  NJ   State Contract  #A81718/Index #G-2004  Total cost of furniture -  $144,871.24  Exemplis, LLC  6415 Katella Ave  Cypress, CA 90630  NJ State Contract #A81711  Total cost of furniture -  $39,654.16  Metal Works, Inc  Dba Metal Works  902 E 4 th   Street  Ludington, MI 49431  NJ State Contract #A81631  Total cost of furniture -  $61,924.78

NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED,   by the West Orange Township Council that the Contract  for   new furniture   and equipment   for the West Orange Public Library   be awarded to the above -  mentioned co mpanies .  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED,   that the Purchasing Agent, on behalf of the Township issue and  execute Purchase Order for the amount   awarded with funds certified available by the Ch ief Financial  Officer.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney , Council President  Adopted:   Decemb er 20, 2022  I certify funds are available in Account No.   03-2645-21-0100-010  $693,064.04  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer /BA

RESOLUTION  Whereas ,   the   Township of West Orange  (formal name of organization)  desires to apply for and obtain a grant from the New Jersey Department of Community Affairs  for approximately   $ 37,209.68   to carry out a project  (dollar amount of   request)  to replace the dugouts at Degnan   P ark   in West Orange , NJ .  (briefly describe the project)  Be it therefore RESOLVED ,  1)   that   the   Township of West Orange  (formal name of organization)  does hereby authorize the application for such a grant;   and,  2)   recognizes and accepts that the Department may offer a lesser or greater amount and therefore,  upon receipt of the grant agreement from the New Jersey Department of Community Affairs, does  further authorize the execution of any such grant agreement; a nd also, upon receipt of the fully  executed agreement from the Department, does further authorize the expenditure of funds pursuant  to the terms of the agreement   between  Township of West Orange  (formal name of organization)  and the New Jersey Department   of Community Affairs.  Be it   further RESOLVED   that the persons whose names, titles, and signatures appear below are  authorized to sign the application, and that they or their successors in said titles are authorized to  sign the agreement, and any other d ocuments necessary in connection therewith:  (signature)   (signature)  Robert D. RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING SERVICE AGREEMENT FOR GOOSE CONTROL TECHNOLOGY - 2023

pages.text: 324-22  December 20, 2022  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   desires   to   enter   into   a  Services   Agreement   with   G-Tech   also   known   as   Goose   Control   Technology   of   NJ   Inc.   ( “ Goose  Control   Technology ” )   to   provide   services   related   to   the   control   of   nuisance-related   Canad ian  G eese   and   other   related   health   issues   at :   (i)   Degnan   Park ;   (ii)   Stagg   Field ;   and   (iii)   the   Oskar  Schindler Performing Arts Center ( collectively   the   “ Sites ” ) ; and whereas  WHEREAS,   th e   proposed   Services   Agreement   (the   “ Agreement ” )   consolidates   the   scope  of services previously entered into with Goose Control Technology as separate agreements; and  WHEREAS,   Goose   Control   Technology   has   been   providing   these   services   to   the  Township for   m ore than   twenty   ( 20 ) years; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   desires   to   retain   Goose   Control   Technology   for   the   year   202 3  to   provide   goose   egg   treatment   at   the   Sites   for   $ 3,000   and   $ 4 50   for   permit s   facilitation/record  keeping,   $4,500   for   nuisance   Canada   goose   re moval,   if   enacted   and   to   coordinate   a   volunteer  nest   site   identification   program   for   a   flat   rate   of   $500,   if   enacted,   for   a   total   contract   amount   not  to exceed $ 8,450 , as set forth in the Agreement annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ; ”   a nd  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Engineer   and   the   Health   Officer   reports   that   Goose   Control  Technology has performed in a satisfactory manner in the past;   and  WHEREAS,   the   contract   price   set   forth   in   the   Agreement   falls   below   the   bid   threshold,  the award of the Agreement is   exempt from the requirements of the Local Public Contracts Law.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   the   annexed   agreement   with  Goose   Control   Technology   fo r   a   term   of   one   (1)   year   during   202 3   and   at   a   rate   not   to   exceed  $ 8,450   for the services as described herein and in the annexed   A greement ; and be it further

RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with   applicable law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C. documents.title: APPROVING AND ENDORSING THE BY LAWS FOR THE DOWNTOWN WEST ORANGE ALLIANCE - 2022

pages.text: 328-22  December 20, 2022  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   Chapter   25,   Section   32.4,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )  created the Special Improvement District within the Township; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   Chapter   25,   Section   32.6   the   Township   Council   designated   the  Downtown   West   Orange   Alliance   (the   “ DWOA ” ),   a   non-profit   corporation,   as   the   District   Management  Corporation for the Special Improvement District within the Township; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   Chapter   25,   Section   32.7   the   Board   of   Trustees   for   the   DWOA   is  empowered   to   “ [a]dopt   and   amend   the   bylaws,   rules,   regulations   and   polices   in   connection   with   the  performance of its duties and the regulation of its affairs; ”   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Board   of   Trustees   has   adopted   on   first   reading,   an   amended   proposed   set   of  byl aws   for   the   DWOA,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ”   and   provided   the   proposed   bylaws   for   the  Township ’ s review, comment, and approval.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   that   the   proposed   amended   bylaws   for   t he   DWOA   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   are   approved  and endorsed by the Township of West Orange.  Karen   J.   Ca rneva le , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council Pre side nt  Adopted:   December 20, 2022  4830-3838-0616, v. 1

1  Downtown W est Orange   Alliance  20 2 3   B udget Narrative  Introduction  Th is   year’s   budget   totals $2 58 ,000 and   provides for an   efficient and effective year full of  planning,   marketing,   promotion,   and organization   strategy .   Th e   revenue   contained in this budget is  derived from   a combination of Special Improvement   Assessments,   Township of West   Orange  c ontributions,   f undraising   e fforts   and   s urplus from 20 2 2 .   We   resum e d   our regular event   schedule  during the 2022 budget year and   welc omed several new businesses to the downtown corridor.   T he  West Orange Street Fair   will be back as well as   a few other events to round out the year. The  downtown West Orange Alliance will continue to   have more in person opportunities to meet   up  downtown.  Revenue  The assessment portion   of this budget   totals   $ 1 33 ,000 .00 1   utilizing a 3 -   tier system   as  sanctioned   by   ordinance 2 . The   tiers   are   based on the type   and location of the   commercial properties  within   the downtown corridor. There   is   a total of   1 8 0   properties 3   that provide commercial space for  over 300 businesses. These businesses consist   of Restaurants, Retail S tores ,   Personal   and Professional  S ervice   c ompanies .  This year’s   income   is broken out   into the following   categories:   T ownship contribution of   C ash  and   In - K ind 4   S ervices ,   Fundraising   e fforts   generated from the Mayors 5K Run   (5K), Street Fair (SF)   and  funds carried from   the   previous year 5 .  Assessment Income   $   1 33 , 0 00 . 00  Township Contribution Cash   $   65 ,809.00  Township Contribution In - Kind   $   28,000.00  Prior Year Surplus   $   5 ,000.00  Fundraising 5K Run   $   5 , 000 .00  Fundraising Efforts   Events   $   29 , 0 91.00  Additional Support Township W. Orange   $   20 , 000 .00  Interest Income   $   100.00  TOTAL   $   2 58 ,000.00  E xpenses  1.   Total   Administ rat ion & Office Operations  1   Estimated assessment amount as of December 20 2 2  2   Ordinance # 1534 - 98  3   Block & Lots   adjustments  4   In - kind = $28,000 for office space and supplies  5   Surplus =   Estimated f unds remaining at the end of 20 2 2

2  Expenses relating to the   Township’s contribution   include   rent,   utilitie s,   telephone, postage,  printing ,   supplies   and   financial   contribution toward s   the   overall budget .   The   Special Improvement  District   (SID)   office   is in   Township Hal l and is part of the in - kind contribution.  Cash   Contribution   $ 65 ,809.00  In - Kind   office   expenses   $28,000.00  Add’l Support Twp   $20 , 000.00  Total   Twp   Contribution   $ 113 ,809.00  Total Cash Contribution   -   Inkind   $85,809.00  Total Administration & Office Operations $   1 3 0 , 00 0 .00 .  Th e administrative and office operation line item   includes   $ 28 , 000   of   In - Kind   expenses received  from   the Township.   The   other   expenses relating   to   Administration and   O ffice   O perations   include  Executive Director salary, payroll fees and taxes, office equipment   a nd supplies.   We continue to look  for   an additional administration staff er as the environment allows .   We have continued to utilize the  software program ;   Member 365   (M365)   which is a sophisticated membership management tool.   The  streamlining benefit of M365 cannot be overemphasized and   has   provide d   a better   organizational tool  for   managing the database of business and property owners .  Administrative Expense  Executive Director   $   60 ,000.00  Administrative Assistant   (estimated)   $   2 5 , 0 00.00  Payroll Fees   $   1 ,000.00  Employer Payroll Taxes   $   8 ,300.00  Sub - Total Admin   Expense   $   94 ,300.00  Office Expenses  Directors Cell Phone   $   6 00.00  Annual Report fees   $   1 00.00  Office Equipment & Software   $   1 , 500 .00  Office Software   $   2 , 50 0.00  Printing ( L etterhead & Brochures)   $   25 0.00  Supplies   & Board Mtg Exp   $   2 , 75 0.00  Sub - Total   Office Expenses   $   7 , 70 0.00  Review  Total Administrative   $   94,300.00  Total Office Expense   $   7 , 7 00.00  In - Kind Support   $   28 ,000.00

3  Total Office   $ 130,000.00  2.   Economic Revitalization (ER) $ 3 ,000.00  M onies budgeted for ER will continue to provide for business retention and recruitment.  T he   ER team   will   continue to   take a more personal approach to meeting with our stakeholders.  The team   has identified key property owners and   locations   within the SID Co rridor   and   will  concentrate   efforts   on   creating   more   retail ready   store fronts and properties. The ER Team will  continue to create an   environment for   recruit ing and   retain ing   business   with in   the downtown  corridor.  3.   Profession al Services &   Insurance   $ 5 , 0 0 0 .00  The   Downtown   WO   Alliance   will   distribute funds for   Professional S ervices   that   include   The  Directors   & Offi cers Insurance ,   Work er ’s Comp ensation Insurance,   Account ing   Services   for   total   budgeted  amount   of $ 5, 0 0 0.00 .  D&O   Insurance   $   1,500.00  Worker’s Compensation   $   500.00  Accounting   $   3 ,000 .00  Total   Professional Services & Ins   $   5 , 0 00.00  4.   Marketing & Promotions $ 77 , 25 0 .00  We will continue to manage several promotional events and fundraisers   that have become  extremely popular   over the years . These include but   are   not limited to   West Orange Street Fair , Downtown  Thursday events,   and the   annual   Holiday Open   House .   The remaining monies in this category   will   support   t he  Mayors 5K ,   ot her   dedicated events   with the goal of   bring ing   p eople into the downtown   c orridor.   Below please  find a breakdown of these expenses.   W e   will   continue to utilize Facebook, Instagram , Zoom   & other  technology resources to promote the downtown corridor   and encourage   people to stay connected.   As well as  promotion through the mobile application and the new Downtown Website.  Tourism Grant Match   $   4,062.50  Marketing Contractor   $   32,400.00  Joseph Fagan   $   18,000.00  Mobile Application   $   3 , 0 00.00  Website Branding Design & M365   $   3, 00 0.00  Street Fair, Downtown Thursdays & others   $   1 6 , 7 87 . 5 0  Total Marketing & Promotions   $   77 , 25 0 .00

4  5.   Organization   $ 6, 60 0 .00  The Organization category provides for the basic structure   of   the Alliance and allows for the  many committees to work together. This line item   permits   the DWOA to promote our programs as well as  the creation for sponsorship opportunities   we need for fundra ising . It is important for the Alliance to have  positive relationships with local organizations and groups such as The Chamber of Commerce,   Friends of  Edison, Friends of the Public Library,   Public Relations Commission ,   the Rotary Club   a nd many   others . It   is a  goal of the Alliance to attend meetings and continue to be a working partner with other organizations. In  addition,   the Alliance will participate in all training and resources the   National   Main Street   o rganization   &  downtownNJ   ha ve   to offer.  Community Events -   OSPAC, WOCC   $   100.00  Dues & Subscriptions   $   1,0 00.00  Main St. NJ   - National Conference   $   5 ,000.00  Trade Shows/Seminars   $   5 00.00  Total Organization   $   6 ,6 0 0.00  6.   Visual Improvement   $6 4 , 15 0.00  W ithin Visual Improvement w e have dedicated   much   of   the   resources to the   design   committee  over the years .   We have made significant improvements to signs,   awnings,   and store fronts.   We have  attracted a   talented team   of individuals that continue to   collaborate   with our stakeholders to improve  the   image of the   downtown corridor   and local store fronts and signs .   This line item will f und way  finding signs,   the holiday   decorations,   and the   popular   façade grant reimbursement program. The  DWOA continues to support maintenance and clean - up of the downtown corridor.   The addition of our  part time litter control person   has worked out very well and we will continue with the extra clean up  help.   Landscaping and beautification of the planters continue to be a priority for the downtown  alliance. Specific support for this category is as   follows.  Banners   $   1 , 5 00.00  Design Improvements   $   3, 65 0.00  Capital Expense & Signage   $   5 ,000.00  Façade Grant Program   $   20,000.00  Holiday Decorations   $   13 , 0 00.00  Landscaping/Streetscape   $   6 ,000.00  Maintenance   &   Supplies   $   1 5 ,000.00  TOTAL   Visual Improvement   $ 6 4 , 15 0.00

5  In summary   The Downtown Alliance   board   is committed to promoting and supporting the   businesses  and property owners along the Main Street Corridor. We will remain   steadfast in evaluating every program  we support and e very dollar we   spend to assure   there is   a   financial   benefit   to our   business and property  owners   a s well as for   the   community   where we live   and work . Orange   $20,000.00  Interest Income   $ 100.00  TOTAL Revenue   $ 2 58 ,000.00  EXPENSES  1.   Admin & Office   O perations   $   130,000.00  2.   Economic Development   $   3 ,000.00  3.   Ins .   & Professional Services   $   5, 0 00.00  4.   Marketing   &   Promotion   $   77 , 25 0.00  5.   O rganization   $   6 ,6 0 0.00  6.   Visual Improvement   $   6 4 , 15 0.00  Total Expenses   $   2 86 ,000.00  In - Kind   office Operations   ($   28,000.00)  TOTAL   Cash   Budget   $   2 58 ,000.00  In Summary, this year’s tot al   p roposed   cash   budget is   $2 58 ,000.00 . I respectfully request that you vote   YES   on  this year’s budget   and fully support the work of the DWOA . Thank you for your attention to this   matter.

documents.title: DWOA BUDGET
AUTHORIZING AWARD OF CONTRACT FOR MUNICIPAL BUILDING INTERIOR IMPROVEMENTS REPAIRS PHASE I TO VMF CONSTRUCTION - 2023

pages.text: 330 -22  December   20, 2022  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   is   seeking   to   retain   a   vendor   to  provide   improvements   and   repairs   to   the   Township   Municipal   Building   at   66   Main   Street,  including,   but   not   limited   to :   (i)   clean ing ,   fix ing,   and   painting   designated   walls ;   (ii)   provid ing  new   interior   flooring   and   wall   base;   (iii)   repairing   and/or   replacing   ceiling   tiles;   (iv)   replacing   all  plumbing   fixtures   in   private   toilet   rooms;   (v)   removing   all   through   window   and/or   wall  mechanical   uni ts   and   all   associated   support   and   mounting;   (vi)   providing   solid   panels   at  windows   and   i nfill   masonry   for   wall   opening   at   exterior   walls;   and   (vii)   providing   window  treatments (collectively the   “ Improvements ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   October   27,   2022,   t he   Township   issued   and   advertised   a   Project  Manual   for   Bids   for   the   Municipal   Building   I nterior   Improvements   and   Repairs   Phase   I,   annexed  hereto as Exhibit   “ A ”   (the   “ Bid Specifications ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   November   15,   2022,   the   Township   issued   an   addendum,   captioned  “ Addendum No. 1 ”   to the Bid Specifications, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ B; ”   and  WHEREAS,   on   November   29,   2022,   the   Township   received   four   (4)   bids   seeking   award  of   the   contract   to   perform   the   Improvements   (the   proposed   “ Contract ” ),   including   the   following  bids:  (i)   VMF   Construction   -   $274,000   base   bid,   $50,000   additional  alternate bid;  (ii)   T.N.S.   Construction,   LLC   -   $278,000   base   bid,   $95,000  additional alternate bid;  (iii)   Skopye   LLC   -   $329,500   base   bid,   $ 76,000   additional  alternate bid; and  (iv)   Grove   Contract ing   LLC   -   $328,500,   $40,000   additional  alternate bid;  and

WHEREAS,   VMF   Construction   with   offices   at   10-09   Utica   Terrace,   Fair   Law,   New  Jersey   ( “ VMF   Construction ” )   submitted   the   lowest   bid,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ C, ”   in  response to the Township ’ s Bid   Specifications;   and  WHEREAS,   on   November   29,   2022,   the   Township   rece ived   a   letter   of   protest   from  T.N.S.   Construction ,   LLC   ( “ TNS ” )   challenging   the   bid   submitted   by   VMF   Group,   annexed  hereto as Exhibit   “ D; ”   and  WHEREAS,   TNS ’ s   challenged:   (i)   VMF   Construct ion ’ s   omission   of   a   certified   financial  statement;   and   (ii)   VMF   Const ruction ’ s   omission   of   a   Public   Works   Certificate   for   its   electrical  subcontractor; and  WHEREAS,   the   alleged   defects   in   TNS ’ s   challenge   do   not   constitute   material   defects   in  VMF   Construct ion ’ s   bid   as:   (i)   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-13(f)   the   Township ’ s   Bid  Specifications   could   not   and   did   not   require   the   submission   of   certified   financial   statements   as  reflected   in   the   bid   submission   checklist   on   page   69   of   the   Bid   Specifications   did   not   i nclude   an  “ x ”   for   “ A   Certified   Financi al   statement   prepared   within   the   last   twelve   months; ”   and   (ii)  N.J.S.A.   34:11-56.55   of   the   Public   Works   Contractor   Registration   Act   requires   that   Public  Works   Certificates   for   a   Contractor ’ s   subcontractors   are   due   “ aft er   the   bid   is   made   and   prior   to  the aw arding of the contract; and  WHEREAS,   VMF   Construction   has   executed   the   Contract,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit  “ E , ”   as   included in the Township ’ s Bid Specifications.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange   that   th e   Township   awards   the   Contract   to   VMF   Construction   as   the   lowest  responsible bidder; and be it further

RESOLVED   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   Contract   with  VMF   Construction,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ E , ”   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby  authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature thereon; and be it further  RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   award   shall   be   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with any applicable law.  Karen   J.   Ca rnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council Pre side nt  Adopted:   December 20, 2022  I hereby certify funds available from  01-2010-00-8052-090  John O. Gross , Chief Financial Officer  4830-3838-0616, v. 1

- 5 -  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE   NOTICE TO BIDDERS MUNICIPAL BUILDING  INTERIOR IMPROVEMENTS  & REPAIRS PHASE I  66 MAIN STREET, West Orange, New Jersey For   The Township of West Orange  Notice is hereby given that sealed bids will be received, opened and read in public by a Township Committee at 66 Main Street, West Orange, NJ 07052 on Tuesday, November 29, 2022 in the Council Chambers, Room 116 at 10AM Local Prevailing Time   for Municipal Building, Interior Improvements & Repairs Phase I, located at 66 Main Street in the Township of West Orange, County of Essex. The major items of construction include, but not   limited to the following:   clean, fix & paint designated walls, provide new interior flooring/ wall base, repair/ replace ceiling tiles,   replace all plumbing fixtures in private toilet rooms, remove   all through window/wall mechanical units and all associated support/mounting & providing solid panels at windows & infill masonry wall opening at exterior walls, & providing window treatment.  The work shall be completed within 120 (One hundred & twenty) calendar days after the date of the “Notice to Proceed” in   accordance with the contract. Sealed proposals will also be accepted by hand delivery or certified mail if they are addressed exactly as follows: Purchasing Agent Township of West Orange Municipal Building 66 Main Street West Orange, NJ 07052 Attn:   Bid Proposal Interior Improvements & Repairs Municipal Building – Phase I  Indicate the time and date of the receipt of bids and bear the name and address of the party submitting the proposal. All proposals submitted by mail or hand delivery must be received by 10:00 AM on Tuesday, November 29, 2022.  Plans, specifications and forms of bids for the proposed work, prepared by Herbst-Musciano LLC, Boonton, New   Jersey have been filed in the office of said Municipal Engineer at 25 Lakeside Avenue, West Orange, New Jersey   and may be inspected by prospective bidders during business hours on or after Monday, October 31, 2022.  Bidders will be furnished with a copy of the plans and specifications by the Municipal Engineer at 25 Lakeside Avenue, West Orange, New Jersey on proper notice and payment of a $100 (One Hundred Dollars)   nonrefundable cost of preparation fee.  A Pre-Bid Conference is scheduled for 11:00 A.M. Local Prevailing Time on Wednesday, November 9, 2022 at the West   Orange Municipal Building, 66 Main Street, West Orange, New Jersey. All General Contractors and Suppliers are   invited to attend. At this time, the Prospective Bidders will have the opportunity to examine the existing site   conditions.  Bids must be enclosed in sealed envelopes, bearing the name and address of the bidder, the name of the job, the time   and date designated for the receipt of bids, addressed to the Purchasing Agent of the Township of West Orange, and   must be accompanied by a Non-Collusion Affidavit and a Certified Check

- 6 -  or Bid Bond for not less than ten percent   (10%) of the amount bid, provided said check not be more than $20,000 nor shall not be less than $500 and   delivered at the place on the hour named above. The Standard Proposal Form and Non-Collusion Affidavit are   attached to the supplemental specifications, copies of which will be furnished on application to the Municipal   Engineer.  The Township of West Orange reserves the right to waive any informalities and to reject any or all bids. No bidder   may with draw his bid within sixty (60) days after the actual date of opening thereof.  The Township of West Orange reserves the right to reject any or all Bids, or to waive any Bid defect where such   defect is not detrimental to the best interest of The Township of West Orange and fairness of the bidding process. The   right is also reserved to increase the quantities specified in the manner designated in the Contract Documents.  ALL CONTRACTORS AND SUB-CONTRACTORS   are required to be registered by the   New Jersey Department of Labor   at the time bids will be received by the Project Owner pursuant to the Public Works Contractor Registration Act (P.L. 2003, c. 91, N.J.S.A. 34:11-56.51). Effective September 1, 2004, ALL Contractors and Sub-Contractors are   also required   to be registered by the New Jersey Department of Treasury,   Division of Revenue at the time bids will be received by the Project Owner pursuant to the Business Registration   Act (P.L. 2004, c. 57, N.J.S.A. 52:32-44). The Contractor and their sub-contractors shall provide the above   certificates with the bid being submitted.  Bidders are required to comply with the requirements of P.L. 1975, c. 127. (NJAC 17:27) (Affirmative Action), of P.L. 1963, c. 150 (Prevailing Wages), and P.L. 1977, c. 33 (Disclosure Statement). By   the order of the Township Council of the Township of West Orange.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk
